body:
This PRD was ratified in the 2026-07-19 design session and confirmed at the subsequent `/to-prd` testing-seam checkpoint. It publishes the selected first PRD of the PRD-ready determination at `reports/cast-member-draft-import-prd-prep.md`; every product decision below was explicitly user-ratified in that session, and no deferred follow-on candidates were ratified. The relevant active authorities are `docs/FOUNDATIONS.md`, `docs/ACTIVE-DOCS.md`, `docs/story-record-schema.md`, and `docs/archival-workflow.md`.

## Problem Statement

The CAST MEMBER record is deliberately the richest record in Continuity Loom: its identity, voice-anchor, pressure-behavior, body-presence, agency, and extended sections are what keep characters from flattening into generic dialogue and generic psychology during prose generation. That richness is also the single largest authoring cost in the app. Creating one dossier means hand-filling roughly fifty prose fields, and the current best workflow lives outside the product: the author pastes `reports/cast-member-record-field-guide.md` and a character dossier into an external LLM, asks for a structured draft, and then manually retypes the response into the editor field by field. That transcription step is slow, error-prone, and discourages creating the rich dossiers the product's own doctrine says active cast deserve.

## Solution

From the Cast Member editor, the author clicks one button that copies a complete, self-contained **Cast Member draft prompt** to the clipboard. They paste it — together with their character dossier — into any external LLM they already use, and the prompt instructs that model to return one fenced JSON object drafting every fillable dossier field, inventing character-fitting, memorable material where the dossier runs out, and declaring its uncertainties and inventions. Back in the editor, the author pastes the response into an import affordance. The app parses it locally, prefills the draft form field by field, and shows an ephemeral three-band import report: what was filled, what was skipped and why, and what still needs the author (always the linked entity selection, plus the model's declared uncertainties and inventions). The author reviews, edits, and saves — or cancels and leaves no trace. The app makes no network or provider call anywhere in this flow; the author remains the only continuity authority and the only writer of the record.

## User Stories

1. As an author, I want a copy button that puts a complete Cast Member draft prompt on my clipboard, so that I can brief an external LLM with a single paste instead of shuttling a separate field guide around
2. As an author, I want the copied prompt to explain the purpose of every Cast Member field, so that the external LLM drafts material a future prose writer can actually use
3. As an author, I want the copied prompt to instruct the LLM to invent character-fitting, memorable material for fields my dossier does not cover, so that imported drafts arrive rich instead of full of holes
4. As an author, I want the copied prompt to demand one fenced JSON object with exact field names plus declared uncertainties and inventions, so that the response can be imported mechanically without reformatting
5. As an author, I want the copied prompt to forbid the LLM from choosing or emitting the linked entity, so that record linkage stays entirely under my control
6. As an author, I want the copied prompt to contain no project records or story data, so that copying it never leaks anything from my project
7. As an author, I want an import affordance on the Cast Member editor that accepts a pasted response, so that I never retype structured output field by field
8. As an author, I want the importer to accept the JSON even when it is wrapped in code fences or surrounded by chat prose, so that a normal assistant reply imports without manual cleanup
9. As an author, I want every valid field imported even when other fields are invalid, so that one bad enum value does not send me back to the external LLM for a full regeneration
10. As an author, I want every skipped field named with its specific reason, so that nothing is ever silently dropped
11. As an author, I want an import report that separates filled fields, skipped fields, and fields needing my attention, so that I know exactly where my review effort belongs
12. As an author, I want the report to surface the LLM's declared uncertainties and invented fields, so that I scrutinize invented material before it can become canon
13. As an author, I want the form's linked entity selection preserved on import, including the pre-bound entity from the linked creation flow, so that an import never breaks record linkage
14. As an author, I want the import affordance available while editing an existing dossier, so that a refreshed external draft can update a character without manual transcription
15. As an author, I want a confirmation listing exactly which fields an import would overwrite when my form already has content, so that I never lose work unknowingly
16. As an author, I want fields absent from the paste left untouched, so that partial drafts merge predictably with what I already wrote
17. As an author, I want nothing saved until I review the prefilled draft and save it myself, so that no external LLM output ever becomes a record automatically
18. As an author, I want the paste, the report, and the unsaved draft to leave no stored residue when I cancel or navigate away, so that rejected assistance output disappears completely
19. As an author, I want the app to make no network or provider call anywhere in this flow, so that my dossier and drafts stay on my machine
20. As an author, I want the draft prompt versioned and mechanically kept in sync with the Cast Member schema, so that newly added schema fields are never silently missing from the prompt

## Implementation Decisions

- **Clipboard round-trip architecture.** The feature is a pure copy-out/paste-back loop. The app compiles nothing from project state into the copied prompt, sends nothing to any provider, and adds no server surface. The external LLM interaction happens entirely outside the app through the author's clipboard.
- **Draft prompt content.** The copied prompt is self-contained: role framing, per-field semantics for every CAST MEMBER schema field (distilled from the retired field guide), extraction-and-invention rules, and a strict output contract — one fenced JSON object using exact schema field names; fields without dossier evidence are invented in character rather than omitted; `entity_id` and unknown keys are never emitted; the response includes an `uncertainties` list and a list of fields the model substantially invented.
- **Invention policy.** The prompt explicitly licenses the external LLM to invent material that fits the dossier's established character and makes them more memorable when the dossier cannot fill a field. This supersedes the evidence-only extraction rules of the retired field guide. Inventions are review targets, not canon: the import report flags them, and nothing enters a record until the author saves.
- **Template ownership and drift protection.** The draft prompt template lives in `@loom/core` as a deterministic, versioned template with its own version identity, separate from the prose template, compiler, and compiler-contract versions, which are untouched. A drift test asserts every CAST MEMBER schema field appears in the template, so a schema change fails the build until the prompt and its authority doc are updated in the same change.
- **Import pipeline.** Parsing, mapping, and report generation are pure functions in `@loom/core`: lenient envelope extraction (accept a fenced JSON block or a JSON object surrounded by prose), per-field validation against the registered CAST MEMBER schema, tolerant per-field acceptance with skip-with-reason accounting, and stripping of `entity_id`, unknown keys, and empty values. No server involvement.
- **Editor affordances and merge semantics.** Copy and Import appear on the Cast Member editor in both create mode (including the linked ENTITY-to-CAST creation flow) and edit mode. Import fills only the fields present in the paste; absent fields stay untouched; the form's `entity_id` is always preserved. When the paste would replace non-empty form values, a confirmation lists the affected fields before anything is overwritten.
- **Import report.** An ephemeral three-band report renders after import: filled fields; skipped fields, each with its named reason (invalid enum value, unknown field, malformed entry, empty value); and needs-author items (the `entity_id` selection, the model's uncertainties, and its invented-fields list). The report is session-ephemeral UI state only.
- **Assistance-output conformance.** The import surface follows the constitution's assistance-output rules: opt-in per invocation, quarantined (the prefilled form is an unsaved draft, clearly not a record), provenance-visible (imported versus invented versus needs-author), and residue-free — no project-store write, no working-set change, no prompt participation, and no persisted browser storage for the paste, the report, or the unsaved draft.
- **Documentation and archival.** A new domain-authority prompt-template document for the Cast Member draft prompt is added under the active-docs registry in the same change, mirroring the repository's existing per-surface prompt-template documents; `reports/cast-member-record-field-guide.md` is archived per `docs/archival-workflow.md` as superseded by it.
- **Constitutional footprint.** No amendment to `docs/FOUNDATIONS.md` is required: the copied prompt is static, record-free template text that reads no assistance source profile, and the import surface complies with the existing optional-LLM-assistance rules. No assistance source profile is added and the compiler contract is unchanged.

### Browser-visible guidance checklist mapping

| Checklist item | PRD home |
| --- | --- |
| `entry point and availability` | Implementation Decisions (editor affordances: create including linked flow, and edit); User Stories 1, 7, 14 |
| `user-visible states, actions, and outcomes` | Solution; Implementation Decisions (merge semantics, import report); User Stories 7-16 |
| `validation, warning, error, and recovery behavior` | Implementation Decisions (import pipeline, import report, merge confirmation); User Stories 9, 10, 15 |
| `prompt preview contents and freshness` | Implementation Decisions (draft prompt content; template ownership and drift protection — the copied text is a static versioned template, fully author-readable, with no compiled project state); User Story 20 |
| `user-initiated external LLM boundary` | Implementation Decisions (clipboard round-trip architecture — no path makes a provider call); User Stories 6, 19 |
| `canon and prose boundary visibility` | Implementation Decisions (assistance-output conformance, invention policy); User Stories 12, 17 |
| `persistence, migration, export, and provenance` | Implementation Decisions (assistance-output conformance — nothing persists except the record the author explicitly saves); User Stories 17, 18 |
| `browser and accessibility regression scenario` | Testing Decisions (editor component seam including keyboard operation and accessible names for the import dialog, report, and confirmation) |

## Testing Decisions

- Tests assert external behavior only: what the author can copy, paste, see, confirm, and save — never internal implementation details.
- `@loom/core` is tested at its pure-function seam: the draft-prompt template (including the drift test asserting every CAST MEMBER schema field appears in the template) and the parse/map/report functions, driven by table-driven fixtures covering valid, partially valid, malformed, and adversarial pastes (bad enums, unknown keys, empty strings, `entity_id` injection, prose-wrapped JSON).
- `@loom/web` is tested at the editor component seam: copy affordance, import dialog, overwrite confirmation, three-band report rendering, `entity_id` preservation in both the standalone and linked creation flows, and keyboard plus accessible-name behavior for the new interactions.
- Prior art: the record-schema and section-model unit tests in `@loom/core`, and the Cast Member editor and record-browser component interaction tests in `@loom/web`.
- No server seam: the feature adds no server behavior.

Seam confirmation: answered — the user ratified the two seams above (primary `@loom/core` pure-function seam; secondary existing `@loom/web` editor component seam; no server seam) at the Step 2 checkpoint in the same session.

## Principles

- `docs/FOUNDATIONS.md` governs this feature as an LLM-assistance surface. The design was checked against §4.1 (user-owned continuity), §9/§9.1 (prompt classes — the copied prompt is static, record-free template text and adds no assistance source profile), §17 (cast dossiers), §26 and §26.1 (assistance output: opt-in, quarantined, provenance-visible, ephemeral, never an automatic mutation), §27 (UI principles), and the §29 alignment checklist, including §29.3 (assistance scope) and §29.9 (assistance scratch leaves no residue). No contradiction and no deliberate exception.
- `docs/ACTIVE-DOCS.md`: the new prompt-template domain authority must be registered in the same change that adds it, per the registry rule.
- `docs/story-record-schema.md` section 5 remains the CAST MEMBER schema authority; this PRD changes no schema.
- `docs/archival-workflow.md` governs the field-guide archival closeout.
- No ADR conflicts: the repository's only ADR (playtest prep-artifact versioning) is untouched by this feature.

## Out of Scope

- Any OpenRouter or in-app LLM call for this feature, including an in-app dossier-drafting assistance surface (explicitly rejected, not deferred).
- Any new assistance source profile, prompt class, or compiler-contract change.
- Structured import for record types other than CAST MEMBER.
- Automatic record creation, mutation, or saving of imported drafts.
- Persisting pastes, import reports, or unsaved drafts anywhere.
- Letting the external LLM choose, emit, or override the linked entity.
- Changes to the prose prompt template, compiler, validation rules, or CAST MEMBER schema.

## Further Notes

- The determination artifact `reports/cast-member-draft-import-prd-prep.md` (tracked and visible on the publication ref) carries the full decision ledger, rejected alternatives, and evidence trail, including the exact ratified filename for the new prompt-template domain authority document, which is pending authoring as part of this PRD's implementation.
- `reports/cast-member-record-field-guide.md` remains the operational tool until this feature ships, then moves to the archive as superseded; its evidence-only extraction rules are superseded by the ratified invention policy.
- External research during the design session (structured-output reliability) supports the two load-bearing choices: JSON as the paste format, and tolerant per-field import, because prompt-only structured output fails a meaningful fraction of the time.
- No deferred follow-on candidates exist: the design session ratified the clipboard round-trip outright and rejected the in-app variant rather than deferring it.
- Labels were explicitly ratified at the Step 2 checkpoint as `enhancement` plus `needs-triage`, matching the repository's house pattern of publishing PRDs for maintainer triage before decomposition into implementation issues.

Seam confirmation: answered — the exact seams named in Testing Decisions were ratified in-session at the Step 2 checkpoint; no seam is open to veto.

labels.description: Maintainer needs to evaluate this issue

title: PRD: Cast Member Dossier Draft Import — Clipboard Round-Trip with Tolerant Per-Field Import
